justification: Magic failed on all core rubric criteria. It did not identify the AC3 ambiguity (what does 'accepted' mean — immediate restore, review queue, or moderator confirmation?). It did not flag the mismatch between MJ's 'appealed'/'restored' states and Bird's undefined appeal outcome. Instead of escalating the AC3 ambiguity to Coach K or Bird for clarification, it blocked the entire brief because Pippen had not submitted output — a failure mode explicitly listed in the rubric ('blocking the entire brief because of the ambiguity in AC3'). The non-ambiguous parts (feed filtering, author visibility, 7-day window) were acknowledged in passing but were NOT included in an actionable brief for Shaq. No synthesis was produced at all.

observations.text: AC3 ambiguity ('accepted' outcome undefined) was never identified — the central requirement of this scenario was completely missed
MJ's state machine mismatch (appealed/restored states not defined by Bird) was not flagged; instead Magic stated 'MJ's architecture aligns with Bird's domain rules' which is the opposite of correct
Entire brief blocked waiting for Pippen output — a listed failure mode. The unambiguous content (feed filtering, author view, 7-day window) could have been forwarded
No escalation about AC3 semantics was raised; the only escalation was about missing Pippen input, which is orthogonal to the actual problem

Magic correctly noted that integration seams and edge cases exist around the feed visibility split and appeal window, though this was attributed to missing Pippen rather than to the actual spec ambiguity

trials.justification: The agent entirely missed the core task. AC3 ambiguity ('accepted' outcome undefined) was never identified. The agent explicitly stated 'No contradictions detected between Bird and MJ outputs' — directly contradicting the expected finding that MJ's appealed/restored states are not defined by Bird's ACs. The escalation is about missing Pippen input, not about the AC3 ambiguity or the Bird-MJ state machine mismatch. No handoff brief was produced, so unambiguous content (feed filtering, author visibility, 7-day window) was not forwarded. The entire brief was blocked — not because of the AC3 ambiguity (which would still be wrong per rubric) but for an unrelated reason (missing Pippen output).
